name: beehiiv-update-subscriber
description: Update an existing Beehiiv subscriber's tier, custom fields, tags, or subscription status. Chain after beehiiv-get-subscriber to use the subscription_id from upstream output.

API_KEY_SECRET.description: Vault path to the Beehiiv API key (e.g. 'beehiiv/api_key').

PUBLICATION_ID.description: Beehiiv publication ID (e.g. 'pub_xxx...'). Supports {{key}} template interpolation.

SUBSCRIPTION_ID.description: The subscription ID to update (e.g. 'sub_xxx...'). Use '{{subscription_id}}' to pull from a preceding beehiiv-get-subscriber node. Supports {{key}} template interpolation.

UNSUBSCRIBE.description: When true, marks the subscription as unsubscribed. This cannot be undone via the API — resubscription requires a new subscription with reactivate_existing=true.

TIER.description: New subscription tier. Leave unset to keep the current tier.

CUSTOM_FIELDS.description: Custom fields to update. Provide as a JSON array of {name, value} objects (e.g. '[{"name":"first_name","value":"Bob"}]'). Only listed fields are updated; others are unchanged. Fields must already exist in the publication.

ADD_TAGS.description: Comma-separated list of tag strings to add to the subscriber (e.g. 'vip,early-adopter'). Max 100 tags per publication. Tags are created automatically if they don't exist.

REMOVE_TAGS.description: Comma-separated list of tag strings to remove from the subscriber (e.g. 'trial'). Non-existent tags are silently ignored.

AUTOMATION_IDS.description: Comma-separated automation IDs to enroll the subscriber in. Each must have an active 'Add by API' trigger.

setup_instructions.title: Get the subscription ID
setup_instructions.detail: Chain a beehiiv-get-subscriber node before this node. Map its output 'subscription_id' field to this node's SUBSCRIPTION_ID config using the template syntax '{{subscription_id}}'.

setup_instructions.title: Configure the update
setup_instructions.detail: Set one or more of TIER, CUSTOM_FIELDS, ADD_TAGS, REMOVE_TAGS, or UNSUBSCRIBE. At least one update field must be specified — a no-op call will still succeed but returns the unchanged subscription.
